title: Varna Boat Rental Guide: How to Choose the Right Boat in 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Varna, the largest city on the Bulgarian Black Sea coast, starts from around €150 for a half-day motorboat and can exceed €1,500 for a crewed luxury yacht. Whether you depart from Port Varna or Marina St. Nikola, BoatHire24 helps you compare verified boat listings to find the perfect vessel for your trip.

Where to set off in Varna

Varna offers several departure points for day trips and longer cruises. The main hub is Port Varna, a commercial and passenger port near the city centre. For a more sheltered start, try Marina St. Nikola, located in the suburb of Euxinograd, known for its fair-weather moorings. The nearby bay of St. Konstantin and Helena is popular for swimming and anchored lunches, while a run south to Kamchia River mouth offers wildlife spotting. For a longer voyage, head east to Cape Kaliakra, about 40 nautical miles north, with dramatic cliffs and a nature reserve.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboats (4-6 guests): Best for coastal cruising and exploring hidden coves near Varna. Half-day rental from €150.
  2. Sailboats (6-8 guests): Ideal for relaxed day sails, often starting from Marina St. Nikola. Full-day from €300.
  3. Catamarans (8-12 guests): Spacious and stable for group tours to Cape Kaliakra or Balchik. Full-day from €700.
  4. Luxury yachts (up to 12 guests): For a premium experience, models like Azimut or Sunseeker are available crewed. Full-day from €1,500.

For a fast and nimble option, consider this speedboat, a Quicksilver Activ 605 Open with 150 HP, ideal for two or three guests exploring the Varna coastline.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Varna?

Costs vary widely by boat type and season, with peak summer rates from June to August.

  • Shared boat tour: from €40 per person for a 3-hour trip.
  • Small motorboat (half-day, 4 guests): from €150.
  • Catamaran (full-day, 10 guests): from €700.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full-day, up to 12 guests): from €1,500.

You can book a boat online in advance to secure the best rates and availability.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Varna?

For most motorboats under a certain power threshold, no licence is required, but the operator will confirm exact requirements. In Bulgaria, a valid ICC or national licence may be needed for larger or more powerful vessels. Always check with the host when booking; BoatHire24 listings provide this information.

Tip: If you are new to boating, book a crewed charter for the day and let a local skipper navigate the winds, such as the afternoon sea breeze that often picks up in Varna Bay during July and August.

At a glance

  • Price range: €150 to €1,500+ per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours to full day
  • Maximum capacity: 2 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Varna

Can I rent a boat in Varna without a licence?

Yes, for many small motorboats you do not need a licence, but you must confirm with the specific listing on BoatHire24 as requirements vary by boat.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Varna?

The best months are June through September when the weather is warm and the sea is calm, with average temperatures above 22°C.

How far is it from Varna to Balchik by boat?

The distance from Varna to Balchik is about 30 nautical miles, which takes roughly 2 hours in a fast motorboat.

Can I book a private boat charter for a group of 10 in Varna?

Yes, catamarans such as the Lagoon 45 or a crewed luxury yacht can accommodate up to 12 guests, bookable directly on BoatHire24.

Whether you are a first-time renter or an experienced boater, Varna's coastline offers endless opportunities for discovery in 2026. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Varna or list your own. Start planning today: browse the listings and find your ideal vessel. If you own a boat, consider listing it with us to reach thousands of travellers.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Varna and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
